_strategy: R4: 2/10 +1 (catalog→front-end NetworkLoss80, orders→front-end NetworkDelay2s). Insight: detector lights on cross-service network impact, not single-pod kill (Coherence cluster failover hides PodKill). R5 strategy: (1) drop PodKill — wasted budget. (2) Strong network chaos in BOTH directions on primary path (loadgen→front-end→carts/catalog/orders→user/payment). (3) Stack Network across 30% pairs to test detector synergy. (4) Skip JVMException — coherence container needs fork stabilization. (5) Try MemoryStress, NetworkCorrupt as new chaos types.
_results: 0/3 +1 (only 3 successful submissions out of 10 candidates due to network-pair-pre-check 500s). v01 front-end→carts NetworkLoss80 → no_anomaly, v04 carts→front-end NetworkDelay3s both → no_anomaly, v08 carts→front-end NetworkCorrupt50 → no_anomaly. Detector signals on R4 exact edges (catalog→front-end Loss80, orders→front-end Delay2s) but flat-lines on rest. Hypothesis: sockshop detector is event-window-specific, not generally responsive to network chaos. Next round: deeper investigation into detector signal source + try edge sweep (every adjacent service pair).

candidates._failure: pair front-end→catalog not in observed_pairs at submit time
pair user→front-end not observed (loadgen does NOT hit user directly; user is an internal service)
memory_size string format not accepted; use int MiB (e.g. 512 not '512MB')
